column5: I utterly failed the 2021 Criterion Challenge not even halfway through the year thanks to ongoing issues with second semester of my school year, then I eventually just lost all motivation. Let's hope the same doesn't happen to me this year, especially not as I start college at the end of summer. I'm not creating a huge watchlist prior, rather I'll choose from each category each weekend to see what interests me the most based on my mood (a change from last year with the hope that my interest is more consistent) For the most part my focus is on watching films that I own a physical copy of since my pile of unwatched Blu-Rays is stacking insanely high. Original list here

1. From the year you were born

Note: Watch Scenes From a Marriage prior
15. A film by a director whose work you haven't seen before
19. Directed by Claire Denis (moved this out of order because my parents wanted to watch a pride month movie with me)
